description: Kafka 4.x KRaft fleet and cluster operations dashboard built on Kafka JMX, kafka_exporter 1.9.0, node metrics, alerts, and VictoriaLogs. Select cls=All for the global fleet view.

panels.description: Consumer membership, committed offsets, progress, and lag. Empty when the cluster has no consumer groups.

targets.expr: label_join(sum by (cls, consumergroup) (clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_consumergroup_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic",consumergroup="$group"}), 0)), "key", " / ", "cls", "consumergroup")
label_join(max by (cls, consumergroup) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_consumergroup_lag{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic",consumergroup="$group"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "consumergroup")

label_join(sum by (cls, consumergroup) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (clamp_min(rate(kafka_consumergroup_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic",consumergroup="$group"}[5m]), 0))), "key", " / ", "cls", "consumergroup")

panels.title: Consumer Group Identity

panels.description: Total unconsumed records for group $group (`kafka:csg_topic:lag` summed over its topics — the same recording rule the KafkaConsumerLagGrowing alert evaluates). Amber ≥10k, orange ≥100k, red ≥1M.

Active members of $group (`kafka_consumergroup_members`, deduplicated). ZERO members while lag exists means consumption has STOPPED — offsets are retained but nobody fetches; lag then grows exactly at the produce rate.

targets.expr: sum(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_consumergroup_members{job="$job",cls="$cls",consumergroup=~"$group"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Distinct topics this group has committed offsets for (`kafka_consumergroup_current_offset` grouped by topic). The Consumed Topics table below breaks these down per topic.

targets.expr: count(count by (topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_consumergroup_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",consumergroup="$group"}))) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Total records committed per second by $group across all its topics (`kafka:csg_topic:commit_rate5m` summed). Zero while lag exists means consumption has stopped entirely.
Observed log-end offset growth and consumer committed-offset progress. Commit progress is not broker network throughput.
panels.title: Append / Commit Rate

panels.description: Per-partition lag of the group (`kafka_consumergroup_lag`, exporter-deduplicated). One flat non-zero lane while the rest drain identifies a stuck partition; uniform growth across lanes means the whole group is under-provisioned; a sawtooth is a batch consumer committing in bursts.
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Partition

panels.description: Alert timeline in the standard Pigsty style: solid bands are firing alerts (`ALERTS{alertstate="firing"}`), pale bands are pending ones still inside their for-window, for every alert carrying this cluster label. Click a legend entry to inspect it in Alertmanager.

Total backlog per consumer group: `kafka:csg_topic:lag` (deduplicated, per-topic recording rule) summed by group, topk $topk. This is the exact series the KafkaConsumerLagGrowing alert evaluates (>100k records and still growing after 30m).
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Group

panels.description: Exact committed offset and lag for every selected consumer group and partition of the selected topic. Empty when no matching consumer groups exist.
panels.title: Consumer Partition Offsets

panels.description: Partition offset positions from kafka_exporter: log end offset (`kafka_topic_partition_current_offset`) vs oldest retained offset (`kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset`). The vertical gap is retained records per partition; parallel slopes mean balanced append load; oldest rising = retention deleting segments.
panels.title: Partition Offsets

panels.description: Per-topic consumption state of this group: partitions, backlog, worst partition lag, commit rate and committed position. Click a topic to open the Kafka Topic dashboard.
panels.title: Consumed Topics

panels.description: kafka_exporter replica liveness (`up{job="kafka",role=""}` per replica): teal while the replica answers scrapes, red when down. Two replicas scrape the same cluster for redundancy and their logical series are deduplicated in every query, so losing one replica does not lose data — losing both blinds topic/partition/consumer panels while brokers keep serving.
panels.title: Exporter Availability

panels.description: kafka_exporter runtime cost: resident memory (`process_resident_memory_bytes`), Go heap in use, scrape duration (right axis, `scrape_duration_seconds`) and samples per scrape. Rising duration tracks growing cardinality; hitting the scrape timeout surfaces as KafkaExporterDown flaps.
panels.title: Exporter Memory / Scrape Cost

targets.expr: job:syslog ip:in(${members:doublequote}) (unit:kafka OR app:kafka) | fields level,ip,unit,_msg

panels.title: Kafka Service Logs
panels.description: Raw Kafka server logs from VictoriaLogs: LogsQL `job:syslog (unit:kafka OR app:kafka)` filtered to the member IPs of this scope (log streams carry node identity, so filtering is by ip, not by kafka cls). Fields: level, ip, unit, message.

title: Kafka Consumer
